extension.description: Manage multiple Agent CLI sessions in a movable VS Code view with real PTYs, session recovery, image input, and communication health monitoring.
viewContainer.title: Agent Terminal
view.sessions.name: Agent Sessions
view.inbox.name: Image Drop Inbox
view.inbox.contextualTitle: Agent Terminal Panel Image Drop Inbox
command.newSession: Create Agent Session with Default Command
command.restoreWorkspaceSessions: Restore Agent Sessions from Previous Window
configuration.title: Agent Terminal Panel
configuration.launchCommand.description: The complete launch command executed by the workspace extension host system shell. You will be prompted to configure it when creating the first session.
configuration.launchProfiles.description: Additional launch profiles shown in the dropdown beside the session add button. Profiles appear in array order and do not replace the default command.
configuration.launchProfiles.name.descri: Menu label and initial name for new sessions.
configuration.launchProfiles.command.des: The complete command executed by the workspace host system shell.
configuration.environment.description: Environment variables added to Agent sessions.
configuration.sessionListPosition.left: Show the session list to the left of the terminal.
configuration.sessionListPosition.right: Show the session list to the right of the terminal.
configuration.sessionListPosition.descri: Position of the session list relative to the terminal.
configuration.startSessionOnOpen.descrip: Automatically create an Agent session when the view first opens. Automatic creation pauses when sessions from the previous window are waiting to be restored.
configuration.terminalImages.enabled.des: Enable Sixel/iTerm terminal images and Codex Pets compatibility by setting TERM=xterm-sixel and removing TERM_PROGRAM. Applies to new or restarted sessions.
configuration.communicationHealth.enable: Show source-aware communication health, traffic, silence duration, and Provider metrics in the session header.
configuration.communicationHealth.sample: Communication health refresh interval in milliseconds. Process network probes may run less frequently.
configuration.communicationHealth.quietT: How long a running session can have no activity from the selected source before being marked quiet.
configuration.communicationHealth.stalle: How long a running session can have no activity before being marked potentially stalled. This is a diagnostic hint, not confirmation of a network failure.
configuration.communicationHealth.proces: Enable process network probing on the workspace host. Linux/WSL/SSH reads TCP bytes, macOS uses nettop, and Windows shows connection counts with PTY-rate fallback.
configuration.communicationHealth.codexS: Extract TTFT and token metadata from local JSONL files already open by monitored Codex processes. Prompt content is never stored, uploaded, or displayed. Linux/WSL/SSH and macOS support precise process mapping.
Maximum number of Agent history records shown for the current workspace.
configuration.sessionHistory.codexComman: Command prefix used to resume or fork Codex history sessions. Fixed arguments are supported.
configuration.sessionHistory.claudeComma: Command prefix used to resume or fork Claude Code history sessions. Fixed arguments are supported.
configuration.notifications.showToast.de: Show approval, waiting-for-input, and completion notifications when the current session is hidden or VS Code is unfocused.
configuration.notifications.completionSo: Play only when the completed session is hidden or VS Code is unfocused.
Sound policy for completed Agent turns.
